title: Repository Detection and Drift Revalidation
rawUserRequest: 完成 Bootstrap Adoption 评审整改执行计划中的 Repository Detection 与 Drift 工单，并走完整 Work Item 闭环。

declaredIntent.summary: 为 Bootstrap Wizard 提供只读、可序列化的对象仓库事实快照，并在最终确认前 fail closed 地检测漂移。

outOfScope: Bootstrap write boundary, dry-run, installer integration, and adoption evidence persistence (WI03-WI04)
Project Doctor, complexity calibration, and formal configuration (WI05-WI08)
Changing business source code, CI policy, or release behavior

sources.reason: Adoption lifecycle and Bootstrap boundary documentation.
WI01 external session API that consumes detection facts.
Existing Git snapshot and remote discovery behavior.
Authoritative repository plan for the Repository Detection and Drift work item.

riskAssessment.reason: The implementation is read-only and fail closed; later write-boundary work must consume its explicit facts rather than infer missing remote or readiness state.

executionDecision.reason: Human confirmation recorded from the user's explicit authorization to execute the full serial remediation plan, including tests, PR, merge, and cleanup; the listed repository-state unknowns are handled as fail-closed facts.

preReviewWarnings: Review that detection performs no writes and that any mismatch blocks confirmation.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record implementation checkpoints for the governed Work Item.

acceptance: detect_repository returns a typed snapshot for a Git repository with at least one commit and raises BootstrapRepositoryError for a non-Git or no-commit path.

The snapshot contains root, commit, branch or detached HEAD, staged/unstaged/untracked paths, remotes with a read URL and a write URL, remote symbolic HEAD when available, local/remote branches, conflict paths, and installed Cockpit presence as explicit fields.
revalidate_repository compares root, branch, commit, dirty paths, remote URLs, remote HEAD, bootstrap base commit, and conflict state, returning every mismatch in a structured fail-closed result.
Detection and drift tests cover clean, dirty, detached, no-commit, remote, conflict, installed-state, and changed-state scenarios without writing to the inspected repository.
The English and Japanese adoption guides explicitly state that detection is read-only, missing remote HEAD remains null, installed state is not readiness, and stale confirmation returns to Review.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Preserve the distinction between detection, confirmation, and later write/evidence Work Items.

intent.problem: WI01 provides an external state machine but no repository-independent fact model for safely deciding whether its confirmation remains current.
constraints: No repository writes; subprocess calls are read-only Git queries.
Detached HEAD, missing remote, dirty paths, and merge conflicts must remain explicit facts.
If remote symbolic HEAD is absent, store null and never infer a default branch.
Conflict paths are recorded but never resolved by detection.
Installed Cockpit is presence only, not adoption readiness or evidence.
intent.rationale: A stable snapshot and explicit drift report let later write-boundary work stop on stale confirmation instead of applying a proposal to changed repository state.

problemStatement: Implement read-only Repository Detection and final Drift Revalidation for Bootstrap Adoption.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Non-Git and no-commit repositories fail closed
Clean, dirty, detached, remote, and conflict facts are captured
Installed Cockpit state is detected without treating it as readiness
Any final repository drift returns a structured blocking result

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized completing the full remediation plan, including governance documentation updates.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore the related tests and documentation.
